design_philosophy.core_loop: Build hospital infrastructure → Attract patients → Diagnose ailments → Treat diseases → Manage finances and reputation → Compete against AI rivals across campaign levels
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Balancing resource constraints (budget, staff capability, room availability) against patient throughput, diagnostic accuracy, and treatment success while managing emergencies and epidemics
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Hospital tycoon: design and operate an efficient medical facility, research exotic treatments for absurd diseases, manage staff personalities and competencies, and outcompete rival hospital chains
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Game enforces consequence-laden systems: neglected maintenance causes explosions killing patients; wrong diagnoses or premature treatments kill patients; epidemic cover-ups risk reputation damage. Players can deliberately overcharge for diagnoses or force risky treatments. Dark humor permeates design philosophy, treating hospital operation as morally ambiguous business simulation rather than altruistic healthcare fantasy

difficulty.continues_system: Campaign structure with individual level objectives; failure results in level restart
difficulty.checkpointing: Time-based level progression: player given build phase before patient arrival; days tracked year-round; emergencies and epidemics occur as timed events
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High agency: player controls hospital policies (diagnosis rates, staff break timing), treatment authorization (can force risky cures), pricing, queue management, and staff deployment. Can deliberately sabotage own hospital through negligence or unethical policies

progression.upgrade_system: Room research system: players unlock new treatment rooms and diagnostic facilities through research; doctors can be trained to specialize in required skills; staff statistics improve through training and experience
progression.unlock_mechanism: Research points generated in Research rooms unlock new room types and treatment improvements; specialist training unlocks doctor capability in specific departments (Operating Theatre, Psychiatry, etc.)
replayability_hooks: Competing AI rivals with distinct personalities (Holly, Deep Thought, etc.) require different strategies
VIP inspection tours offer cash/reputation bonuses, incentivizing reputation management
Epidemics and emergencies present dynamic challenge scenarios requiring tactical response
Multiple difficulty implicit in level design progression
Player can experiment with unethical hospital policies (overcharging, forced treatments) creating emergent narratives

world_building_techniques: Fictitious disease names and descriptions (Bloaty Head, King Complex, Alien DNA) establish absurdist tone
Pop culture references throughout game world establish 1990s context
AI rival naming conventions (references to Red Dwarf, Hitchhiker's Guide) signal game's comedic irreverence
Staff personalities and statistics create implied narrative of workplace dynamics
Epidemic events and health inspector interventions create dramatic moments within simulation framework
Advisor commentary provides ambient worldbuilding through reactive dialogue

faction_indicators: Hospital reputation score reflecting player's ethical choices
Staff morale and turnover indicating workplace management quality
Patient demographics and treatment outcomes revealing hospital tier/specialization
danger_signals: Epidemic cover-up attempts signal willingness to deceive regulators
Excessive diagnostic overcharging (>100%) reveals predatory pricing strategies
High patient mortality rates from forced experimental treatments indicate reckless operation
Staff dismissal patterns may indicate labor exploitation
Rat infestations and maintenance negligence signal institutional neglect
environmental_storytelling: Hospital layout and room distribution reflects player's prioritization (fancy reception vs. adequate treatment capacity)
Staff room presence/quality indicates workplace consideration
Plant and furniture placement in corridors signals attention to patient experience
Equipment maintenance state visible through decay/explosions reveals operational philosophy
Queue lengths and patient wait times reflect systemic efficiency or dysfunction
